data.text: I'll start by reading the key files mentioned in the bead description to understand what needs to be deleted.

input.description: Survey files to delete/modify for routing cleanup
input.prompt:
Survey the following files and return their contents (or confirmation they exist/don't exist). This is for a cleanup task that will delete deprecated routing config fields and flags from a Go CLI project.

Files to check:
1. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/cli/internal/config/types.go
2. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/cli/internal/config/config.go
3. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json
4. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/cli/internal/config/routing_migration_test.go
5. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go
6. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go
7. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
8. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/cli/cmd/agent_route_status.go
9. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/cli/internal/server/workers.go (lines 620-850)
10. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/docs/adaptive-min-tier.md
11. /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3bd7396a-20260429T220335-335a5db3/docs/migrations/routing-config.md

Also list any files matching these patterns:
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop*.go
- cli/*_escalation_*_test.go
- cli/**/profile_ladder_test.go

For each file, show the full content or at minimum 100+ lines so I can understand what to keep vs delete.

104	// ResolveNoProgressCooldown returns the effective no-progress cooldown for

108			return defaultNoProgressCooldown

255	// RoutingConfig is the agent routing policy block. See FEAT-006 Profile
256	// Routing and epic ddx-bbb65768. ProfilePriority is the legacy flat list
257	// and is deprecated in favour of ProfileLadders (per-profile tier lists).
258	// When both are present, ProfileLadders wins.
259	//
260	// Note: agent.routing.default_harness was REMOVED in the routing-config
261	// deprecation pass (bead ddx-87fb72c2). The top-level agent.harness field
262	// is now a tie-break preference for harness selection, not a default
263	// override. Configs that still carry default_harness fail to load with a
264	// migration message; see docs/migrations/routing-config.md.
265	type RoutingConfig struct {
266		// ProfilePriority is the deprecated flat-list form. New configs should
267		// use ProfileLadders.
268		ProfilePriority []string `yaml:"profile_priority,omitempty" json:"profile_priority,omitempty"`
269		// ProfileLadders maps a profile name to the ordered tier list that a
270		// dispatch should try. Opt-in: only consulted when the operator passes
271		// `--escalate`. The default execute path does not read this map.
272		// Example:
273		//   default: [cheap, standard, smart]
274		//   cheap:   [cheap]
275		//   fast:    [fast, smart]
276		//   smart:   [smart]
277		ProfileLadders map[string][]string `yaml:"profile_ladders,omitempty" json:"profile_ladders,omitempty"`
278		// ModelOverrides maps a profile name to a concrete model reference.
279		// Opt-in: only consulted when the operator passes `--override-model`.
280		// e.g. { "cheap": "qwen/qwen3.6", "smart": "claude-opus-4-6" }.
281		ModelOverrides map[string]string `yaml:"model_overrides,omitempty" json:"model_overrides,omitempty"`
282	}

284	var defaultProfileLadders = map[string][]string{

286		"cheap":   {"cheap"},
287		"fast":    {"fast", "smart"},
288		"smart":   {"smart"},
289	}
290	
291	// DefaultProfileLadders returns the built-in profile escalation policy.
292	func DefaultProfileLadders() map[string][]string {
293		out := make(map[string][]string, len(defaultProfileLadders))
294		for profile, ladder := range defaultProfileLadders {
295			out[profile] = append([]string(nil), ladder...)
296		}
297		return out
298	}
299	
300	// ResolvedLadder returns the escalation ladder for the named profile. If
301	// ProfileLadders contains an entry for profile, that wins. Otherwise falls
302	// back to the deprecated ProfilePriority for the default profile only. If
303	// neither is set, returns the shipped FEAT-006 profile ladder.
304	func (r *RoutingConfig) ResolvedLadder(profile string) []string {
305		if profile == "" {
306			profile = "default"
307		}
308		if r == nil {
309			if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
310				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
311			}
312			return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
313		}
314		if r.ProfileLadders != nil {
315			if ladder, ok := r.ProfileLadders[profile]; ok && len(ladder) > 0 {
316				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
317			}
318		}
319		if profile == "default" && len(r.ProfilePriority) > 0 {
320			return append([]string(nil), r.ProfilePriority...)
321		}
322		if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
323			return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
324		}
325		return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
326	}

455			c.Bead = &BeadConfig{}
456		}
457		if c.PersonaBindings == nil {
458			c.PersonaBindings = make(map[string]string)
459		}
460		if c.UpdateCheck == nil {
461			c.UpdateCheck = &UpdateCheckConfig{
462				Enabled:   true,
463				Frequency: "24h",
464			}
465		} else {
466			if c.UpdateCheck.Frequency == "" {
467				c.UpdateCheck.Frequency = "24h"
468			}
469		}
470	}

13	
14	// TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness covers AC #1 of bead
15	// ddx-87fb72c2: a config carrying agent.routing.default_harness must
16	// fail to load with a migration message. The CLI surfaces the error to
17	// the user; the loader's exit code is non-zero (the *cobra.Command path
18	// returns the error to its caller).
19	func TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness(t *testing.T) {
20		tempDir := t.TempDir()
21		ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
22		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
23		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
24	library:
25	  path: "./library"
26	  repository:
27	    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
28	    branch: "main"
29	agent:
30	  routing:
31	    default_harness: claude
32	`), 0644))
33	
34		_, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
35		require.Error(t, err, "loading must fail when agent.routing.default_harness is present")
36	
37		migErr, ok := err.(*RoutingMigrationError)
38		require.True(t, ok, "expected RoutingMigrationError, got %T: %v", err, err)
39		assert.Equal(t, "agent.routing.default_harness", migErr.Field)
40	
41		msg := err.Error()
42		assert.Contains(t, msg, "agent.routing.default_harness")
43		assert.Contains(t, msg, "removed")
44		assert.Contains(t, msg, "docs/migrations/routing-config.md")
45	}
46	
47	// TestLoadConfigWarnsOnceForOptInRoutingFields covers AC #2: configs
48	// containing profile_ladders + model_overrides load successfully but
49	// emit a one-time process warning, AND the default execute path does
50	// not consult those fields. Consultation only happens when --escalate /
51	// --override-model are explicitly passed.
52	func TestLoadConfigWarnsOnceForOptInRoutingFields(t *testing.T) {
53		ResetRoutingDeprecationWarnings()
54		t.Cleanup(ResetRoutingDeprecationWarnings)
55	
56		tempDir := t.TempDir()
57		ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
58		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
59		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
60	library:
61	  path: "./library"
62	  repository:
63	    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
64	    branch: "main"
65	agent:
66	  routing:
67	    profile_ladders:
68	      default: [cheap, standard, smart]
69	    model_overrides:
70	      cheap: qwen/qwen3.6
71	`), 0644))
72	
73		oldStderr := os.Stderr
74		r, w, err := os.Pipe()
75		require.NoError(t, err)
76		os.Stderr = w
77		t.Cleanup(func() { os.Stderr = oldStderr })
78	
79		cfg, loadErr := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
80		require.NoError(t, loadErr)
81		require.NotNil(t, cfg)
82	
83		// Calling load a second time must NOT re-emit the same warning.
84		_, _ = LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
85	
86		require.NoError(t, w.Close())
87		os.Stderr = oldStderr
88		out, err := io.ReadAll(r)
89		require.NoError(t, err)
90		stderrStr := string(out)
91	
92		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in")
93		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "agent.routing.model_overrides is opt-in")
94		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "--escalate")
95		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "--override-model")
96		// Second load does not duplicate the warning lines.
97		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(stderrStr, "agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in"),
98			"profile_ladders warning must fire once per process")
99		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(stderrStr, "agent.routing.model_overrides is opt-in"),
100			"model_overrides warning must fire once per process")
101	
102		// Config still parses these fields so opt-in flags can consult them.
103		require.NotNil(t, cfg.Agent)
104		require.NotNil(t, cfg.Agent.Routing)
105		require.Contains(t, cfg.Agent.Routing.ProfileLadders, "default")
106		assert.Equal(t, "qwen/qwen3.6", cfg.Agent.Routing.ModelOverrides["cheap"])
107	}

1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"sort"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
12	)
13	
14	// AdaptiveMinTierThreshold is the cheap-tier trailing success rate below which
15	// AdaptiveMinTier recommends skipping the cheap tier. At 0.20, four out of
16	// five cheap-tier attempts must fail before the cheap tier is suppressed.
17	const AdaptiveMinTierThreshold = 0.20
18	
19	// AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples is the minimum number of cheap-tier attempts
20	// required in the window before the cheap-tier success rate is considered
21	// statistically meaningful. Below this the cheap tier is kept in-range so
22	// we do not starve it on insufficient evidence.
23	const AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples = 3
24	
25	// DefaultAdaptiveMinTierWindow is the default trailing window size used by
26	// execute-loop when --adaptive-min-tier-window is not specified.
27	const DefaultAdaptiveMinTierWindow = 50
28	
29	// adaptiveResetFileName is the name of the reset marker file written under
30	// .ddx/agent-logs/ by WriteAdaptiveResetMarker.
31	const adaptiveResetFileName = "adaptive-reset.json"
32	
33	// AdaptiveResetMarker is persisted to .ddx/agent-logs/adaptive-reset.json
34	// when the operator runs `ddx agent route-status reset`. AdaptiveMinTier
35	// ignores execution results whose directory timestamp predates ResetAt,
36	// returning the adaptive metric to a clean baseline without deleting history.
37	type AdaptiveResetMarker struct {
38		ResetAt time.Time `json:"reset_at"`
39	}
40	
41	// ReadAdaptiveResetMarker loads the reset marker from workingDir, returning
42	// nil when no marker file exists or when the file is unreadable or corrupt.
43	func ReadAdaptiveResetMarker(workingDir string) *AdaptiveResetMarker {
44		path := filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "agent-logs", adaptiveResetFileName)
45		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
46		if err != nil {
47			return nil
48		}
49		var m AdaptiveResetMarker
50		if json.Unmarshal(data, &m) != nil {
51			return nil
52		}
53		return &m
54	}
55	
56	// WriteAdaptiveResetMarker writes a reset marker timestamped to now into
57	// workingDir/.ddx/agent-logs/. Returns the absolute path of the file written.
58	// Subsequent AdaptiveMinTier calls will ignore executions whose directory
59	// timestamp predates the marker, effectively clearing the trailing window.
60	func WriteAdaptiveResetMarker(workingDir string) (string, error) {
61		dir := filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "agent-logs")
62		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
63			return "", err
64		}
65		path := filepath.Join(dir, adaptiveResetFileName)
66		data, err := json.Marshal(AdaptiveResetMarker{ResetAt: time.Now().UTC()})
67		if err != nil {
68			return "", err
69		}
70		if err := os.WriteFile(path, data, 0o644); err != nil {
71			return "", err
72		}
73		return path, nil
74	}
75	
76	// AdaptiveMinTierInfraFailureModes contains failure_mode values that indicate
77	// infrastructure or configuration failures that did not reach an actual model
78	// invocation. Cheap-tier attempts with these failure modes are excluded from
79	// the success-rate computation so that infra outages and misconfiguration do
80	// not permanently condemn cheap tier after the root cause is resolved.
81	var AdaptiveMinTierInfraFailureModes = map[string]bool{
82		"no_viable_provider":    true,
83		"harness_not_installed": true,
84	}
85	
86	// AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics carries the full state of the adaptive min-tier
87	// computation. Used by 'ddx agent route-status --adaptive' to surface enough
88	// information to debug a "why is cheap being skipped?" scenario without
89	// reading source code.
90	type AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics struct {
91		// WindowSize is the trailing-window size passed to the computation.
92		WindowSize int
93		// TotalInWindow is the count of all tier-classified attempts in the window
94		// after reset filtering (any tier).
95		TotalInWindow int
96		// CheapAttempts is the count of cheap-tier attempts that contributed to
97		// the success-rate calculation (infra failures excluded).
98		CheapAttempts int
99		// CheapSuccesses is the count of cheap-tier attempts with outcome==task_succeeded.
100		CheapSuccesses int
101		// CheapInfraSkipped is the count of cheap-tier attempts excluded because
102		// their failure_mode is in AdaptiveMinTierInfraFailureModes.
103		CheapInfraSkipped int
104		// CheapSuccessRate is CheapSuccesses/CheapAttempts, or 0 when CheapAttempts==0.
105		CheapSuccessRate float64
106		// Threshold is the success-rate boundary below which cheap tier is suppressed.
107		Threshold float64
108		// MinSamples is the minimum sample count required before the rate is
109		// considered statistically meaningful.
110		MinSamples int
111		// EffectiveFloor is the recommended minimum tier (TierCheap or TierStandard).
112		EffectiveFloor ModelTier
113		// IsSkippingCheap is true when cheap tier is currently suppressed.
114		IsSkippingCheap bool
115		// ResetAt is the time of the most recent reset marker, or zero when none.
116		ResetAt time.Time
117	}
118	
119	// SuccessStatus mirrors agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess. The agent package's
120	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guard catches drift if either side
121	// renames. Defined locally so escalation does not import agent.
122	const SuccessStatus = "success"
123	
124	// EscalatableStatuses is the set of executor status strings that warrant
125	// retrying with a higher tier. Mirrors a subset of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*
126	// strings; the agent package has a TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab
127	// guard (see agent/tier_escalation_alignment_test.go) to catch drift.
128	var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
129		"execution_failed":             true,
130		"post_run_check_failed":        true,
131		"land_conflict":                true,
132		"structural_validation_failed": true,
133	}
134	
135	// AdaptiveMinTierResult carries the recommendation from AdaptiveMinTier along
136	// with the observed cheap-tier sample count and success rate so callers can
137	// emit a log line explaining the decision.
138	type AdaptiveMinTierResult struct {
139		// Tier is the recommended minimum tier: TierCheap when the cheap tier is
140		// viable, TierStandard when cheap-tier success is below the threshold.
141		Tier ModelTier
142		// CheapAttempts is the number of cheap-tier attempts observed in the
143		// window. Zero when no cheap-tier history was found.
144		CheapAttempts int
145		// CheapSuccessRate is CheapSuccesses / CheapAttempts, or 0 when
146		// CheapAttempts is 0.
147		CheapSuccessRate float64
148		// Skipped is true when the recommendation is to skip the cheap tier.
149		Skipped bool
150	}
151	
152	// taskResultLite is a minimal projection of agent.ExecuteBeadResult that
153	// captures only the fields AdaptiveMinTier needs. Defined locally so the
154	// escalation package does not depend on the agent package.
155	type taskResultLite struct {
156		Harness     string `json:"harness"`
157		Model       string `json:"model"`
158		Outcome     string `json:"outcome"`
159		FailureMode string `json:"failure_mode,omitempty"`
160	}
161	
162	// TierResolver resolves a (harness, tier) pair to a concrete model name. This
163	// is normally agent.ResolveModelTier; injected as a parameter so the
164	// escalation package does not import agent.
165	type TierResolver func(harness string, tier ModelTier) string
166	
167	// AdaptiveMinTier inspects the most recent `window` entries under
168	// workingDir/.ddx/executions/*/result.json, computes the cheap-tier trailing
169	// success rate, and returns a recommendation:
170	//
171	//   - When cheap-tier success rate < AdaptiveMinTierThreshold (and at least
172	//     AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples cheap attempts were observed), returns
173	//     TierStandard with Skipped=true.
174	//   - Otherwise returns TierCheap with Skipped=false.
175	//
176	// A tier is identified by resolving (harness, model) back through the
177	// supplied resolver — attempts whose harness/model do not match any known
178	// tier mapping are ignored. Infrastructure failures (failure_mode in
179	// AdaptiveMinTierInfraFailureModes) are excluded from the success-rate
180	// calculation. Executions before a reset marker are ignored. When
181	// workingDir has no executions directory (fresh project) the cheap tier
182	// is kept in-range so a first run is not artificially restricted.
183	func AdaptiveMinTier(workingDir string, window int, resolver TierResolver) AdaptiveMinTierResult {
184		diag := computeAdaptiveDiagnostics(workingDir, window, resolver)
185		return AdaptiveMinTierResult{
186			Tier:             diag.EffectiveFloor,
187			CheapAttempts:    diag.CheapAttempts,
188			CheapSuccessRate: diag.CheapSuccessRate,
189			Skipped:          diag.IsSkippingCheap,
190		}
191	}
192	
193	// ComputeAdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics returns the full diagnostic state for the
194	// adaptive min-tier computation. Callers use this to power the
195	// 'ddx agent route-status --adaptive' display without re-implementing the
196	// traversal logic.
197	func ComputeAdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics(workingDir string, window int, resolver TierResolver) AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics {
198		return computeAdaptiveDiagnostics(workingDir, window, resolver)
199	}
200	
201	// parseDirTimestamp parses the timestamp prefix from an execution directory
202	// name (format YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS-<hex>) into a UTC time. Returns false when
203	// the name does not match the expected format.
204	func parseDirTimestamp(name string) (time.Time, bool) {
205		if len(name) < 15 {
206			return time.Time{}, false
207		}
208		t, err := time.Parse("20060102T150405", name[:15])
209		if err != nil {
210			return time.Time{}, false
211		}
212		return t.UTC(), true
213	}
214	
215	func computeAdaptiveDiagnostics(workingDir string, window int, resolver TierResolver) AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics {
216		diag := AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics{
217			WindowSize:     window,
218			Threshold:      AdaptiveMinTierThreshold,
219			MinSamples:     AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples,
220			EffectiveFloor: TierCheap,
221		}
222	
223		if m := ReadAdaptiveResetMarker(workingDir); m != nil {
224			diag.ResetAt = m.ResetAt
225		}
226	
227		execRoot := filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "executions")
228		entries, err := os.ReadDir(execRoot)
229		if err != nil {
230			return diag
231		}
232	
233		names := make([]string, 0, len(entries))
234		for _, entry := range entries {
235			if entry.IsDir() {
236				names = append(names, entry.Name())
237			}
238		}
239		// Directory names are sortable timestamps (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS-<hash>), so
240		// lexicographic sort is chronological.
241		sort.Strings(names)
242	
243		type attempt struct {
244			tier    ModelTier
245			success bool
246			infra   bool // excluded from success rate: infra/config failure
247		}
248		collected := make([]attempt, 0, len(names))
249		for _, name := range names {
250			// Skip executions that predate the reset marker.
251			if !diag.ResetAt.IsZero() {
252				if ts, ok := parseDirTimestamp(name); ok && ts.Before(diag.ResetAt) {
253					continue
254				}
255			}
256	
257			resultPath := filepath.Join(execRoot, name, "result.json")
258			raw, err := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
259			if err != nil {
260				continue
261			}
262			var res taskResultLite
263			if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &res); err != nil {
264				continue
265			}
266			if res.Harness == "" {
267				continue
268			}
269			tier := classifyAttemptTier(res.Harness, res.Model, resolver)
270			if tier == "" {
271				continue
272			}
273			collected = append(collected, attempt{
274				tier:    tier,
275				success: res.Outcome == "task_succeeded",
276				infra:   AdaptiveMinTierInfraFailureModes[res.FailureMode],
277			})
278		}
279		if window > 0 && len(collected) > window {
280			collected = collected[len(collected)-window:]
281		}
282	
283		diag.TotalInWindow = len(collected)
284		var cheapAttempts, cheapSuccesses, cheapInfraSkipped int
285		for _, a := range collected {
286			if a.tier != TierCheap {
287				continue
288			}
289			if a.infra {
290				cheapInfraSkipped++
291				continue
292			}
293			cheapAttempts++
294			if a.success {
295				cheapSuccesses++
296			}
297		}
298		diag.CheapAttempts = cheapAttempts
299		diag.CheapSuccesses = cheapSuccesses
300		diag.CheapInfraSkipped = cheapInfraSkipped
301	
302		if cheapAttempts > 0 {
303			diag.CheapSuccessRate = float64(cheapSuccesses) / float64(cheapAttempts)
304		}
305		if cheapAttempts >= AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples && diag.CheapSuccessRate < AdaptiveMinTierThreshold {
306			diag.EffectiveFloor = TierStandard
307			diag.IsSkippingCheap = true
308		}
309		return diag
310	}
311	
312	// classifyAttemptTier returns the ModelTier that corresponds to a (harness,
313	// model) pair by reverse-lookup against the supplied resolver. Returns ""
314	// when no tier in TierOrder matches — e.g. an ad-hoc model pin not present
315	// in the catalog, which should not contribute to tier-level analytics. A
316	// nil resolver is treated as no-match for every tier.
317	func classifyAttemptTier(harness, model string, resolver TierResolver) ModelTier {
318		if harness == "" || resolver == nil {
319			return ""
320		}
321		for _, tier := range TierOrder {
322			if resolver(harness, tier) == model {
323				return tier
324			}
325		}
326		return ""
327	}
328	
329	// TierOrder defines the escalation sequence from cheapest to most capable.
330	var TierOrder = []ModelTier{TierCheap, TierStandard, TierSmart}
331	
332	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
333	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
334	// locking out a provider for too long.
335	//
336	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
337	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
338	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
339	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
340	
341	// tierIndex returns the position of t in TierOrder, or -1 if not found.
342	func tierIndex(t ModelTier) int {
343		for i, tier := range TierOrder {
344			if tier == t {
345				return i
346			}
347		}
348		return -1
349	}
350	
351	// TiersInRange returns the subset of TierOrder from minTier to maxTier inclusive.
352	// Empty string defaults to the global extremes (cheap and smart).
353	// If minTier > maxTier in the order, an empty slice is returned.
354	func TiersInRange(minTier, maxTier ModelTier) []ModelTier {
355		if minTier == "" {
356			minTier = TierCheap
357		}
358		if maxTier == "" {
359			maxTier = TierSmart
360		}
361		minIdx := tierIndex(minTier)
362		maxIdx := tierIndex(maxTier)
363		if minIdx < 0 {
364			minIdx = 0
365		}
366		if maxIdx < 0 {
367			maxIdx = len(TierOrder) - 1
368		}
369		if minIdx > maxIdx {
370			return nil
371		}
372		// Return a copy so callers cannot mutate TierOrder.
373		out := make([]ModelTier, maxIdx-minIdx+1)
374		copy(out, TierOrder[minIdx:maxIdx+1])
375		return out
376	}
377	
378	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
379	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
380	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
381	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
382		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
383	}
384	
385	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
386	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
387		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
388		if probeResult != "" {
389			body += " probe=" + probeResult
390		}
391		if detail != "" {
392			body += "\n" + detail
393		}
394		return body
395	}
396	
397	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
398	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
399	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
400	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
401	
402	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
403	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
404	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
405	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
406	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
407		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
408		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
409		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
410		Status     string  `json:"status"`
411		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
412		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
413	}
414	
415	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
416	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
417	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
418	type EscalationSummary struct {
419		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
420		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
421		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
422		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
423	}
424	
425	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
426	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
427	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
428	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
429	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
430	// SuccessStatus.
431	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
432		out := EscalationSummary{
433			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
434			WinningTier:    winningTier,
435		}
436		if out.WinningTier == "" {
437			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
438		}
439		for _, a := range attempts {
440			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
441			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
442				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
443			}
444		}
445		return out
446	}
447	
448	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
449	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
450	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
451	type BeadEventAppender interface {
452		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
453	}
454	
455	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
456	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
457	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
458	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
459	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
460	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
461		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
462			return nil
463		}
464		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
465		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
466		if err != nil {
467			return err
468		}
469		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
470			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
471			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
472			Body:      string(body),
473			Actor:     actor,
474			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
475			CreatedAt: createdAt,
476		})
477	}

1	# Adaptive Min-Tier
2	
3	The adaptive min-tier feature automatically promotes the minimum routing tier
4	from `cheap` to `standard` when cheap-tier attempts have been consistently
5	failing, protecting the execution queue from burning time and budget on attempts
6	that are unlikely to succeed.
7	
8	## How it works
9	
10	Each time `ddx agent execute-loop` (or `ddx work`) claims a bead, it reads the
11	most recent N execution results from `.ddx/executions/*/result.json` and
12	computes the cheap-tier trailing success rate:
13	
14	```
15	cheap_success_rate = cheap_successes / cheap_attempts  (within trailing window)
16	```
17	
18	If all of the following are true, the cheap tier is skipped for this run:
19	- `cheap_attempts >= 3` (AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples) — enough evidence
20	- `cheap_success_rate < 0.20` (AdaptiveMinTierThreshold) — rate below threshold
21	
22	When the cheap tier is skipped, a log line is emitted:
23	
24	```
25	adaptive min-tier: skipping cheap tier (trailing success rate 0.00 over 9 attempts; threshold 0.20) — min-tier=standard
26	```
27	
28	### Infra failure exclusion
29	
30	Attempts whose `failure_mode` indicates an infrastructure or configuration
31	failure that did not reach an actual model invocation are **excluded** from the
32	success-rate computation:
33	
34	| `failure_mode`          | Excluded? | Reason                                         |
35	|-------------------------|-----------|------------------------------------------------|
36	| `no_viable_provider`    | Yes       | No route could be resolved; model never called |
37	| `harness_not_installed` | Yes       | Harness binary missing; model never called      |
38	| All others              | No        | Model was invoked; outcome is real signal       |
39	
40	This prevents a configuration outage (e.g., a missing harness binary or a
41	routing misconfiguration) from permanently condemning cheap tier after the root
42	cause is resolved.
43	
44	## Metric store
45	
46	| Location | Format | Purpose |
47	|----------|--------|---------|
48	| `.ddx/executions/{attempt-id}/result.json` | JSON object, one per execution | Primary source for adaptive computation |
49	| `.ddx/agent-logs/routing-outcomes.jsonl` | JSONL, append-only | Harness-level analytics (latency, cost, success rate) — **not** used by adaptive min-tier |
50	| `.ddx/agent-logs/adaptive-reset.json` | JSON object | Reset marker written by `route-status reset` |
51	
52	### `result.json` schema (fields read by adaptive min-tier)
53	
54	```json
55	{
56	  "harness":      "claude",
57	  "model":        "claude-haiku-4-5",
58	  "outcome":      "task_succeeded | task_failed | task_no_changes",
59	  "failure_mode": "no_viable_provider | harness_not_installed | ..."
60	}
61	```
62	
63	Executions with an empty `harness` field are already excluded (these represent
64	attempts where no harness could be resolved at all).
65	
66	### `adaptive-reset.json` schema
67	
68	```json
69	{ "reset_at": "2026-04-29T16:27:02Z" }
70	```
71	
72	After a reset, `AdaptiveMinTier` ignores every execution whose directory
73	timestamp (`YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS-<hex>`) predates `reset_at`. Historical data is
74	preserved on disk; only the adaptive view of it changes.
75	
76	## Diagnosing "why is cheap being skipped?"
77	
78	Run:
79	
80	```
81	ddx agent route-status --adaptive
82	```
83	
84	Example output:
85	
86	```
87	Adaptive Min-Tier State
88	--------------------------------------------------
89	window size:      50 attempts
90	reset at:         (none)
91	total in window:  12 (all tiers)
92	cheap attempts:   9 (contributing to success rate)
93	infra-skipped:    3 (excluded: no_viable_provider / harness_not_installed)
94	cheap successes:  0
95	success rate:     0.00  (threshold: 0.20, min-samples: 3)
96	effective floor:  standard  [cheap tier is SKIPPED — success rate below threshold]
97	
98	To reset: ddx agent route-status reset --yes
99	```
100	
101	For JSON output (useful for scripting):
102	
103	```
104	ddx agent route-status --adaptive --json
105	```
106	
107	## Resetting the adaptive metric
108	
109	When the underlying problem has been fixed (e.g., a misconfigured model name
110	corrected, a missing harness installed, or a quota restored), reset the trailing
111	window to give cheap tier a fresh start:
112	
113	```
114	ddx agent route-status reset --yes
115	```
116	
117	This writes `.ddx/agent-logs/adaptive-reset.json` with the current timestamp.
118	The output names every file touched:
119	
120	```
121	Adaptive min-tier state cleared.
122	
123	Touched: /path/to/project/.ddx/agent-logs/adaptive-reset.json
124	
125	Cheap-tier will now be evaluated from a clean baseline.
126	Run 'ddx agent route-status --adaptive' to verify the new state.
127	```
128	
129	After the reset, the next `execute-loop` invocation evaluates cheap tier on its
130	own merits. No prior trailing-window verdict carries over.
131	
132	Without `--yes`, the command prints what it would do and exits cleanly (no
133	changes made):
134	
135	```
136	ddx agent route-status reset
137	```
138	
139	```
140	This will write a reset marker that causes the adaptive min-tier logic
141	to ignore all execution history recorded before this moment.
142	Cheap-tier eligibility will be re-evaluated on its own merits.
143	
144	Re-run with --yes to confirm:
145	  ddx agent route-status reset --yes
146	```
147	
148	## Bypassing vs. resetting
149	
150	| Mechanism | Effect | Use case |
151	|-----------|--------|----------|
152	| `--no-adaptive-min-tier` | Permanently disables adaptive gating for that run | Testing; known-good environments where you never want gating |
153	| `route-status reset --yes` | Clears trailing-window state; gating re-activates from scratch | After fixing the root cause of cheap-tier failures |
154	
155	Prefer reset over permanent bypass so the adaptive guard can re-activate if
156	failures recur.
157	
158	## Configuration flags (on `execute-loop`)
159	
160	| Flag | Default | Description |
161	|------|---------|-------------|
162	| `--adaptive-min-tier-window` | 50 | Number of most-recent attempts in the trailing window |
163	| `--no-adaptive-min-tier` | false | Disable adaptive gating entirely for this run |
164	| `--min-tier` | (adaptive) | Pin the minimum tier; overrides adaptive evaluation |
165

1	# Migration: routing-config deprecation (bead ddx-87fb72c2)
2	
3	This guide covers the breaking change to `agent.routing.*` in
4	`.ddx/config.yaml`. The change ships as part of epic `ddx-fdd3ea36`
5	(endpoint-first routing).
6	
7	## Summary
8	
9	| Field                              | Status         | Action                       |
10	| ---------------------------------- | -------------- | ---------------------------- |
11	| `agent.routing.default_harness`    | **Removed**    | Delete the field             |
12	| `agent.routing.profile_ladders`    | Opt-in only    | Consulted only with `--escalate`        |
13	| `agent.routing.model_overrides`    | Opt-in only    | Consulted only with `--override-model`  |
14	| `agent.harness` (top-level)        | Kept           | Tie-break preference, not a default override |
15	| `agent.routing.profile_priority`   | Already deprecated | Still parsed; warns at load |
16	
17	## `agent.routing.default_harness` → removed
18	
19	The field has been deleted from the schema. DDx now rejects configs that
20	still carry it with a hard error at startup and a pointer back to this
21	guide. Exit code is non-zero.
22	
23	### Before
24	
25	```yaml
26	agent:
27	  harness: claude
28	  routing:
29	    default_harness: agent
30	```
31	
32	### After
33	
34	```yaml
35	agent:
36	  harness: claude
37	```
38	
39	The top-level `agent.harness` field is preserved and remains honored as
40	a tie-break preference during route resolution. It is **not** a silent
41	default override.
42	
43	## `agent.routing.profile_ladders` → opt-in via `--escalate`
44	
45	The default execute path no longer iterates the ladder. A single
46	`ResolveRoute` call drives each attempt; `profile_ladders` is consulted
47	only when the operator explicitly passes `--escalate` to enable
48	tier-ladder escalation semantics.
49	
50	If your config still sets `profile_ladders`, DDx emits a one-time
51	process warning at config-load time:
52	
53	```
54	warning: agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in.
55	It is consulted only when --escalate is passed; the default execute
56	path ignores it. See docs/migrations/routing-config.md.
57	```
58	
59	To silence the warning either drop the field, or pass `--escalate` on
60	the runs where you want tier escalation.
61	
62	## `agent.routing.model_overrides` → opt-in via `--override-model`
63	
64	The same treatment as `profile_ladders`. The default execute path
65	ignores `model_overrides`. Pass the new `--override-model` flag to opt
66	into per-tier override consultation:
67	
68	```
69	ddx agent execute-loop --escalate --override-model
70	```
71	
72	A one-time process warning fires at config-load time when the field is
73	set without invocation.
74	
75	## Test commands
76	
77	```bash
78	# Reject a config with the removed field:
79	echo 'version: "1.0"
80	library:
81	  path: ./library
82	  repository:
83	    url: https://example.com/repo
84	    branch: main
85	agent:
86	  routing:
87	    default_harness: claude' > /tmp/bad.yaml
88	DDX_PROJECT_ROOT=/tmp ddx status   # exits non-zero with migration message
89	```
90	
91	## Server worker path alignment (ddx-c7081f89)
92	
93	The server worker path (`ddx work` without `--local`) now matches the CLI path
94	semantics described above. Prior to bead `ddx-c7081f89`, the worker started
95	escalation whenever neither harness nor model was pinned. After that bead,
96	`WorkerSpec.Escalate` must be `true` for the tier loop to engage — identical to
97	the `--escalate` flag on the CLI path. The `--escalate` flag is forwarded to the
98	server worker spec automatically when passed to `ddx work`.
99	
100	## Background
101	
102	Prior to this change, three competing surfaces dictated harness
103	selection: the top-level `agent.harness` field, the routing block's
104	`default_harness`, and the live route-resolution call graph. The
105	endpoint-first redesign (epic ddx-fdd3ea36) collapses harness selection
106	into a single explicit decision per dispatch: route resolution is the
107	source of truth, and `agent.harness` is the operator's tie-break. The
108	ladder + overrides knobs remain available for operators who explicitly
109	want escalation or model substitution, but they are no longer
110	consulted by default.
111

30	// Routing test seams (ddx-755f5881 AC #2): record every RouteRequest issued
31	// by the default `ddx agent run` path so unit tests can assert that the
32	// default path calls ResolveRoute exactly once with Profile: "default" and
33	// no other fields. Production code never reads this slice; it is exported
34	// only for tests.
35	var (
36		routeRequestRecorderMu sync.Mutex
37		routeRequestRecorder   []agentlib.RouteRequest
38	)
39	
40	func recordRouteRequestForTest(req agentlib.RouteRequest) {
41		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
42		routeRequestRecorder = append(routeRequestRecorder, req)
43		routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
44	}
45	
46	// RecordedRouteRequestsForTest returns a copy of all RouteRequests recorded
47	// by the agent CLI surface since the last reset.
48	func RecordedRouteRequestsForTest() []agentlib.RouteRequest {
49		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
50		defer routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
51		out := make([]agentlib.RouteRequest, len(routeRequestRecorder))
52		copy(out, routeRequestRecorder)
53		return out
54	}
55	
56	// ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest clears the route-request recording slice.
57	func ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest() {
58		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
59		routeRequestRecorder = nil
60		routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
61	}

129			{
130				name:    "escalate cheap to standard",
131				profile: "default",
132				statuses: map[escalation.ModelTier]string{
133					"cheap":    ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
134					"standard": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
135				},
136				want:  []escalation.ModelTier{"cheap", "standard"},
137				final: "standard",
138			},
139			{
140				name:    "escalate all tiers then exhaustion",
141				profile: "default",
142				statuses: map[escalation.ModelTier]string{
143					"cheap":    ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
144					"standard": ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict,
145					"smart":    ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed,
146				},
147				want:  []escalation.ModelTier{"cheap", "standard", "smart"},
148				final: "smart",
149			},
150			{
151				name:    "max tier caps ladder",
152				profile: "default",
153				maxTier: "standard",
154				statuses: map[escalation.ModelTier]string{
155					"cheap":    ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
156					"standard": ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
157				},
158				want:  []escalation.ModelTier{"cheap", "standard"},
159				final: "standard",
160			},
161			{
162				name:    "cheap profile never escalates",
163				profile: "cheap",
164				statuses: map[escalation.ModelTier]string{
165					"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
166				},
167				want:  []escalation.ModelTier{"cheap"},
168				final: "cheap",
169			},
170		}
171	
172		for _, tc := range cases {
173			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
174				got, final := simulateProfileLadder(tc.profile, tc.maxTier, tc.statuses)
175				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, got)
176				assert.Equal(t, tc.final, final)
177			})
178		}

181	func TestDefaultProfileRepresentativeRunMostlyClosesAtCheap(t *testing.T) {
182		representative := []map[escalation.ModelTier]string{
183			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
184			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
185			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
186			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
187			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
188			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
189			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, "standard": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
190			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed, "standard": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
191			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict, "standard": ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, "smart": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
192			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed, "standard": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
193		}
194	
195		cheapClosures := 0
196		for _, statuses := range representative {
197			_, final := simulateProfileLadder("default", "", statuses)
198			if final == escalation.TierCheap {
199				cheapClosures++
200			}
201		}
202	
203		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, cheapClosures, len(representative)/2)
204	}
205	
206	func simulateProfileLadder(profile, maxTier string, statuses map[escalation.ModelTier]string) ([]escalation.ModelTier, escalation.ModelTier) {
207		ladder := ResolveProfileLadder(nil, profile, "", maxTier)
208		attempted := []escalation.ModelTier{}
209		var final escalation.ModelTier
210		for _, tier := range ladder {
211			attempted = append(attempted, tier)
212			final = tier
213			status := statuses[tier]
214			if status == "" || status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || !escalation.ShouldEscalate(status) {
215				break
216			}
217		}
218		return attempted, final
219	}

253	}
254	
255	// RoutingConfig is the agent routing policy block. See FEAT-006 Profile
256	// Routing and epic ddx-bbb65768. ProfilePriority is the legacy flat list
257	// and is deprecated in favour of ProfileLadders (per-profile tier lists).
258	// When both are present, ProfileLadders wins.
259	//
260	// Note: agent.routing.default_harness was REMOVED in the routing-config
261	// deprecation pass (bead ddx-87fb72c2). The top-level agent.harness field
262	// is now a tie-break preference for harness selection, not a default
263	// override. Configs that still carry default_harness fail to load with a
264	// migration message; see docs/migrations/routing-config.md.
265	type RoutingConfig struct {
266		// ProfilePriority is the deprecated flat-list form. New configs should
267		// use ProfileLadders.
268		ProfilePriority []string `yaml:"profile_priority,omitempty" json:"profile_priority,omitempty"`
269		// ProfileLadders maps a profile name to the ordered tier list that a
270		// dispatch should try. Opt-in: only consulted when the operator passes
271		// `--escalate`. The default execute path does not read this map.
272		// Example:
273		//   default: [cheap, standard, smart]
274		//   cheap:   [cheap]
275		//   fast:    [fast, smart]
276		//   smart:   [smart]
277		ProfileLadders map[string][]string `yaml:"profile_ladders,omitempty" json:"profile_ladders,omitempty"`
278		// ModelOverrides maps a profile name to a concrete model reference.
279		// Opt-in: only consulted when the operator passes `--override-model`.
280		// e.g. { "cheap": "qwen/qwen3.6", "smart": "claude-opus-4-6" }.
281		ModelOverrides map[string]string `yaml:"model_overrides,omitempty" json:"model_overrides,omitempty"`
282	}
283	
284	var defaultProfileLadders = map[string][]string{
285		"default": {"cheap", "standard", "smart"},
286		"cheap":   {"cheap"},
287		"fast":    {"fast", "smart"},
288		"smart":   {"smart"},
289	}
290	
291	// DefaultProfileLadders returns the built-in profile escalation policy.
292	func DefaultProfileLadders() map[string][]string {
293		out := make(map[string][]string, len(defaultProfileLadders))
294		for profile, ladder := range defaultProfileLadders {
295			out[profile] = append([]string(nil), ladder...)
296		}
297		return out
298	}
299	
300	// ResolvedLadder returns the escalation ladder for the named profile. If
301	// ProfileLadders contains an entry for profile, that wins. Otherwise falls
302	// back to the deprecated ProfilePriority for the default profile only. If
303	// neither is set, returns the shipped FEAT-006 profile ladder.
304	func (r *RoutingConfig) ResolvedLadder(profile string) []string {
305		if profile == "" {
306			profile = "default"
307		}
308		if r == nil {
309			if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
310				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
311			}
312			return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
313		}
314		if r.ProfileLadders != nil {
315			if ladder, ok := r.ProfileLadders[profile]; ok && len(ladder) > 0 {
316				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
317			}
318		}
319		if profile == "default" && len(r.ProfilePriority) > 0 {
320			return append([]string(nil), r.ProfilePriority...)
321		}
322		if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
323			return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
324		}
325		return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
326	}

390	// DefaultNewConfig returns a new config with default values applied

394			Library: &LibraryConfig{

396				Repository: &RepositoryConfig{
397					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
398					Branch: "main",
399				},
400			},
401			PersonaBindings: make(map[string]string),

412	// GetMetaPrompt returns the meta-prompt path, defaulting to focused.md if unset
413	// Returns empty string if explicitly set to null/empty (disabled)
414	func (c *NewConfig) GetMetaPrompt() string {
415		if c.System == nil || c.System.MetaPrompt == nil {
416			// Unset: return default
417			return "claude/system-prompts/focused.md"
418		}
419		// Explicitly set (could be empty string to disable)
420		return *c.System.MetaPrompt
421	}

423	// ApplyDefaults ensures all required fields have default values
424	func (c *NewConfig) ApplyDefaults() {
425		if c.Version == "" {
426			c.Version = "1.0"
427		}
428		if c.Library == nil {
429			c.Library = &LibraryConfig{
430				Path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx",
431				Repository: &RepositoryConfig{
432					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
433					Branch: "main",
434				},
435			}
436		} else {
437			if c.Library.Path == "" {
438				c.Library.Path = ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
439			}
440			if c.Library.Repository == nil {
441				c.Library.Repository = &RepositoryConfig{
442					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
443					Branch: "main",
444				}
445			} else {
446				if c.Library.Repository.URL == "" {
447					c.Library.Repository.URL = "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library"
448				}
449				if c.Library.Repository.Branch == "" {
450					c.Library.Repository.Branch = "main"
451				}
452			}
453		}
454		if c.Bead == nil {
455			c.Bead = &BeadConfig{}
456		}
457		if c.PersonaBindings == nil {
458			c.PersonaBindings = make(map[string]string)
459		}
460		if c.UpdateCheck == nil {
461			c.UpdateCheck = &UpdateCheckConfig{
462				Enabled:   true,
463				Frequency: "24h",
464			}
465		} else {
466			if c.UpdateCheck.Frequency == "" {
467				c.UpdateCheck.Frequency = "24h"
468			}
469		}
470	}

1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"sort"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
12	)
13	
14	// AdaptiveMinTierThreshold is the cheap-tier trailing success rate below which
15	// AdaptiveMinTier recommends skipping the cheap tier. At 0.20, four out of
16	// five cheap-tier attempts must fail before the cheap tier is suppressed.
17	const AdaptiveMinTierThreshold = 0.20
18	
19	// AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples is the minimum number of cheap-tier attempts
20	// required in the window before the cheap-tier success rate is considered
21	// statistically meaningful. Below this the cheap tier is kept in-range so
22	// we do not starve it on insufficient evidence.
23	const AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples = 3
24	
25	// DefaultAdaptiveMinTierWindow is the default trailing window size used by
26	// execute-loop when --adaptive-min-tier-window is not specified.
27	const DefaultAdaptiveMinTierWindow = 50
28	
29	// adaptiveResetFileName is the name of the reset marker file written under
30	// .ddx/agent-logs/ by WriteAdaptiveResetMarker.
31	const adaptiveResetFileName = "adaptive-reset.json"
32	
33	// AdaptiveResetMarker is persisted to .ddx/agent-logs/adaptive-reset.json
34	// when the operator runs `ddx agent route-status reset`. AdaptiveMinTier
35	// ignores execution results whose directory timestamp predates ResetAt,
36	// returning the adaptive metric to a clean baseline without deleting history.
37	type AdaptiveResetMarker struct {
38		ResetAt time.Time `json:"reset_at"`
39	}
40	
41	// ReadAdaptiveResetMarker loads the reset marker from workingDir, returning
42	// nil when no marker file exists or when the file is unreadable or corrupt.
43	func ReadAdaptiveResetMarker(workingDir string) *AdaptiveResetMarker {
44		path := filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "agent-logs", adaptiveResetFileName)
45		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
46		if err != nil {
47			return nil
48		}
49		var m AdaptiveResetMarker
50		if json.Unmarshal(data, &m) != nil {
51			return nil
52		}
53		return &m
54	}
55	
56	// WriteAdaptiveResetMarker writes a reset marker timestamped to now into
57	// workingDir/.ddx/agent-logs/. Returns the absolute path of the file written.
58	// Subsequent AdaptiveMinTier calls will ignore executions whose directory
59	// timestamp predates the marker, effectively clearing the trailing window.
60	func WriteAdaptiveResetMarker(workingDir string) (string, error) {
61		dir := filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "agent-logs")
62		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
63			return "", err
64		}
65		path := filepath.Join(dir, adaptiveResetFileName)
66		data, err := json.Marshal(AdaptiveResetMarker{ResetAt: time.Now().UTC()})
67		if err != nil {
68			return "", err
69		}
70		if err := os.WriteFile(path, data, 0o644); err != nil {
71			return "", err
72		}
73		return path, nil
74	}
75	
76	// AdaptiveMinTierInfraFailureModes contains failure_mode values that indicate
77	// infrastructure or configuration failures that did not reach an actual model
78	// invocation. Cheap-tier attempts with these failure modes are excluded from
79	// the success-rate computation so that infra outages and misconfiguration do
80	// not permanently condemn cheap tier after the root cause is resolved.
81	var AdaptiveMinTierInfraFailureModes = map[string]bool{
82		"no_viable_provider":    true,
83		"harness_not_installed": true,
84	}
85	
86	// AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics carries the full state of the adaptive min-tier
87	// computation. Used by 'ddx agent route-status --adaptive' to surface enough
88	// information to debug a "why is cheap being skipped?" scenario without
89	// reading source code.
90	type AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics struct {
91		// WindowSize is the trailing-window size passed to the computation.
92		WindowSize int
93		// TotalInWindow is the count of all tier-classified attempts in the window
94		// after reset filtering (any tier).
95		TotalInWindow int
96		// CheapAttempts is the count of cheap-tier attempts that contributed to
97		// the success-rate calculation (infra failures excluded).
98		CheapAttempts int
99		// CheapSuccesses is the count of cheap-tier attempts with outcome==task_succeeded.
100		CheapSuccesses int
101		// CheapInfraSkipped is the count of cheap-tier attempts excluded because
102		// their failure_mode is in AdaptiveMinTierInfraFailureModes.
103		CheapInfraSkipped int
104		// CheapSuccessRate is CheapSuccesses/CheapAttempts, or 0 when CheapAttempts==0.
105		CheapSuccessRate float64
106		// Threshold is the success-rate boundary below which cheap tier is suppressed.
107		Threshold float64
108		// MinSamples is the minimum sample count required before the rate is
109		// considered statistically meaningful.
110		MinSamples int
111		// EffectiveFloor is the recommended minimum tier (TierCheap or TierStandard).
112		EffectiveFloor ModelTier
113		// IsSkippingCheap is true when cheap tier is currently suppressed.
114		IsSkippingCheap bool
115		// ResetAt is the time of the most recent reset marker, or zero when none.
116		ResetAt time.Time
117	}
118	
119	// SuccessStatus mirrors agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess. The agent package's
120	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guard catches drift if either side
121	// renames. Defined locally so escalation does not import agent.
122	const SuccessStatus = "success"
123	
124	// EscalatableStatuses is the set of executor status strings that warrant
125	// retrying with a higher tier. Mirrors a subset of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*
126	// strings; the agent package has a TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab
127	// guard (see agent/tier_escalation_alignment_test.go) to catch drift.
128	var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
129		"execution_failed":             true,
130		"post_run_check_failed":        true,
131		"land_conflict":                true,
132		"structural_validation_failed": true,
133	}
134	
135	// AdaptiveMinTierResult carries the recommendation from AdaptiveMinTier along
136	// with the observed cheap-tier sample count and success rate so callers can
137	// emit a log line explaining the decision.
138	type AdaptiveMinTierResult struct {
139		// Tier is the recommended minimum tier: TierCheap when the cheap tier is
140		// viable, TierStandard when cheap-tier success is below the threshold.
141		Tier ModelTier
142		// CheapAttempts is the number of cheap-tier attempts observed in the
143		// window. Zero when no cheap-tier history was found.
144		CheapAttempts int
145		// CheapSuccessRate is CheapSuccesses / CheapAttempts, or 0 when
146		// CheapAttempts is 0.
147		CheapSuccessRate float64
148		// Skipped is true when the recommendation is to skip the cheap tier.
149		Skipped bool
150	}
151	
152	// taskResultLite is a minimal projection of agent.ExecuteBeadResult that
153	// captures only the fields AdaptiveMinTier needs. Defined locally so the
154	// escalation package does not depend on the agent package.
155	type taskResultLite struct {
156		Harness     string `json:"harness"`
157		Model       string `json:"model"`
158		Outcome     string `json:"outcome"`
159		FailureMode string `json:"failure_mode,omitempty"`
160	}
161	
162	// TierResolver resolves a (harness, tier) pair to a concrete model name. This
163	// is normally agent.ResolveModelTier; injected as a parameter so the
164	// escalation package does not import agent.
165	type TierResolver func(harness string, tier ModelTier) string
166	
167	// AdaptiveMinTier inspects the most recent `window` entries under
168	// workingDir/.ddx/executions/*/result.json, computes the cheap-tier trailing
169	// success rate, and returns a recommendation:
170	//
171	//   - When cheap-tier success rate < AdaptiveMinTierThreshold (and at least
172	//     AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples cheap attempts were observed), returns
173	//     TierStandard with Skipped=true.
174	//   - Otherwise returns TierCheap with Skipped=false.
175	//
176	// A tier is identified by resolving (harness, model) back through the
177	// supplied resolver — attempts whose harness/model do not match any known
178	// tier mapping are ignored. Infrastructure failures (failure_mode in
179	// AdaptiveMinTierInfraFailureModes) are excluded from the success-rate
180	// calculation. Executions before a reset marker are ignored. When
181	// workingDir has no executions directory (fresh project) the cheap tier
182	// is kept in-range so a first run is not artificially restricted.
183	func AdaptiveMinTier(workingDir string, window int, resolver TierResolver) AdaptiveMinTierResult {
184		diag := computeAdaptiveDiagnostics(workingDir, window, resolver)
185		return AdaptiveMinTierResult{
186			Tier:             diag.EffectiveFloor,
187			CheapAttempts:    diag.CheapAttempts,
188			CheapSuccessRate: diag.CheapSuccessRate,
189			Skipped:          diag.IsSkippingCheap,
190		}
191	}
192	
193	// ComputeAdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics returns the full diagnostic state for the
194	// adaptive min-tier computation. Callers use this to power the
195	// 'ddx agent route-status --adaptive' display without re-implementing the
196	// traversal logic.
197	func ComputeAdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics(workingDir string, window int, resolver TierResolver) AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics {
198		return computeAdaptiveDiagnostics(workingDir, window, resolver)
199	}
200	
201	// parseDirTimestamp parses the timestamp prefix from an execution directory
202	// name (format YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS-<hex>) into a UTC time. Returns false when
203	// the name does not match the expected format.
204	func parseDirTimestamp(name string) (time.Time, bool) {
205		if len(name) < 15 {
206			return time.Time{}, false
207		}
208		t, err := time.Parse("20060102T150405", name[:15])
209		if err != nil {
210			return time.Time{}, false
211		}
212		return t.UTC(), true
213	}
214	
215	func computeAdaptiveDiagnostics(workingDir string, window int, resolver TierResolver) AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics {
216		diag := AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics{
217			WindowSize:     window,
218			Threshold:      AdaptiveMinTierThreshold,
219			MinSamples:     AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples,
220			EffectiveFloor: TierCheap,
221		}
222	
223		if m := ReadAdaptiveResetMarker(workingDir); m != nil {
224			diag.ResetAt = m.ResetAt
225		}
226	
227		execRoot := filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "executions")
228		entries, err := os.ReadDir(execRoot)
229		if err != nil {
230			return diag
231		}
232	
233		names := make([]string, 0, len(entries))
234		for _, entry := range entries {
235			if entry.IsDir() {
236				names = append(names, entry.Name())
237			}
238		}
239		// Directory names are sortable timestamps (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS-<hash>), so
240		// lexicographic sort is chronological.
241		sort.Strings(names)
242	
243		type attempt struct {
244			tier    ModelTier
245			success bool
246			infra   bool // excluded from success rate: infra/config failure
247		}
248		collected := make([]attempt, 0, len(names))
249		for _, name := range names {
250			// Skip executions that predate the reset marker.
251			if !diag.ResetAt.IsZero() {
252				if ts, ok := parseDirTimestamp(name); ok && ts.Before(diag.ResetAt) {
253					continue
254				}
255			}
256	
257			resultPath := filepath.Join(execRoot, name, "result.json")
258			raw, err := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
259			if err != nil {
260				continue
261			}
262			var res taskResultLite
263			if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &res); err != nil {
264				continue
265			}
266			if res.Harness == "" {
267				continue
268			}
269			tier := classifyAttemptTier(res.Harness, res.Model, resolver)
270			if tier == "" {
271				continue
272			}
273			collected = append(collected, attempt{
274				tier:    tier,
275				success: res.Outcome == "task_succeeded",
276				infra:   AdaptiveMinTierInfraFailureModes[res.FailureMode],
277			})
278		}
279		if window > 0 && len(collected) > window {
280			collected = collected[len(collected)-window:]
281		}
282	
283		diag.TotalInWindow = len(collected)
284		var cheapAttempts, cheapSuccesses, cheapInfraSkipped int
285		for _, a := range collected {
286			if a.tier != TierCheap {
287				continue
288			}
289			if a.infra {
290				cheapInfraSkipped++
291				continue
292			}
293			cheapAttempts++
294			if a.success {
295				cheapSuccesses++
296			}
297		}
298		diag.CheapAttempts = cheapAttempts
299		diag.CheapSuccesses = cheapSuccesses
300		diag.CheapInfraSkipped = cheapInfraSkipped
301	
302		if cheapAttempts > 0 {
303			diag.CheapSuccessRate = float64(cheapSuccesses) / float64(cheapAttempts)
304		}
305		if cheapAttempts >= AdaptiveMinTierMinSamples && diag.CheapSuccessRate < AdaptiveMinTierThreshold {
306			diag.EffectiveFloor = TierStandard
307			diag.IsSkippingCheap = true
308		}
309		return diag
310	}
311	
312	// classifyAttemptTier returns the ModelTier that corresponds to a (harness,
313	// model) pair by reverse-lookup against the supplied resolver. Returns ""
314	// when no tier in TierOrder matches — e.g. an ad-hoc model pin not present
315	// in the catalog, which should not contribute to tier-level analytics. A
316	// nil resolver is treated as no-match for every tier.
317	func classifyAttemptTier(harness, model string, resolver TierResolver) ModelTier {
318		if harness == "" || resolver == nil {
319			return ""
320		}
321		for _, tier := range TierOrder {
322			if resolver(harness, tier) == model {
323				return tier
324			}
325		}
326		return ""
327	}
328	
329	// TierOrder defines the escalation sequence from cheapest to most capable.
330	var TierOrder = []ModelTier{TierCheap, TierStandard, TierSmart}
331	
332	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
333	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
334	// locking out a provider for too long.
335	//
336	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
337	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
338	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
339	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
340	
341	// tierIndex returns the position of t in TierOrder, or -1 if not found.
342	func tierIndex(t ModelTier) int {
343		for i, tier := range TierOrder {
344			if tier == t {
345				return i
346			}
347		}
348		return -1
349	}
350	
351	// TiersInRange returns the subset of TierOrder from minTier to maxTier inclusive.
352	// Empty string defaults to the global extremes (cheap and smart).
353	// If minTier > maxTier in the order, an empty slice is returned.
354	func TiersInRange(minTier, maxTier ModelTier) []ModelTier {
355		if minTier == "" {
356			minTier = TierCheap
357		}
358		if maxTier == "" {
359			maxTier = TierSmart
360		}
361		minIdx := tierIndex(minTier)
362		maxIdx := tierIndex(maxTier)
363		if minIdx < 0 {
364			minIdx = 0
365		}
366		if maxIdx < 0 {
367			maxIdx = len(TierOrder) - 1
368		}
369		if minIdx > maxIdx {
370			return nil
371		}
372		// Return a copy so callers cannot mutate TierOrder.
373		out := make([]ModelTier, maxIdx-minIdx+1)
374		copy(out, TierOrder[minIdx:maxIdx+1])
375		return out
376	}
377	
378	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
379	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
380	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
381	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
382		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
383	}
384	
385	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
386	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
387		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
388		if probeResult != "" {
389			body += " probe=" + probeResult
390		}
391		if detail != "" {
392			body += "\n" + detail
393		}
394		return body
395	}
396	
397	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
398	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
399	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
400	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
401	
402	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
403	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
404	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
405	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
406	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
407		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
408		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
409		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
410		Status     string  `json:"status"`
411		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
412		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
413	}
414	
415	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
416	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
417	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
418	type EscalationSummary struct {
419		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
420		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
421		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
422		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
423	}
424	
425	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
426	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
427	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
428	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
429	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
430	// SuccessStatus.
431	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
432		out := EscalationSummary{
433			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
434			WinningTier:    winningTier,
435		}
436		if out.WinningTier == "" {
437			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
438		}
439		for _, a := range attempts {
440			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
441			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
442				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
443			}
444		}
445		return out
446	}
447	
448	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
449	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
450	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
451	type BeadEventAppender interface {
452		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
453	}
454	
455	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
456	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
457	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
458	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
459	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
460	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
461		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
462			return nil
463		}
464		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
465		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
466		if err != nil {
467			return err
468		}
469		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
470			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
471			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
472			Body:      string(body),
473			Actor:     actor,
474			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
475			CreatedAt: createdAt,
476		})
477	}

14	// TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness covers AC #1 of bead
15	// ddx-87fb72c2: a config carrying agent.routing.default_harness must
16	// fail to load with a migration message. The CLI surfaces the error to

18	// returns the error to its caller).
19	func TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness(t *testing.T) {
20		tempDir := t.TempDir()
21		ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
22		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
23		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
24	library:
25	  path: "./library"
26	  repository:
27	    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
28	    branch: "main"
29	agent:
30	  routing:
31	    default_harness: claude
32	`), 0644))
33	
34		_, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
35		require.Error(t, err, "loading must fail when agent.routing.default_harness is present")
36	
37		migErr, ok := err.(*RoutingMigrationError)
38		require.True(t, ok, "expected RoutingMigrationError, got %T: %v", err, err)
39		assert.Equal(t, "agent.routing.default_harness", migErr.Field)
40	
41		msg := err.Error()
42		assert.Contains(t, msg, "agent.routing.default_harness")
43		assert.Contains(t, msg, "removed")
44		assert.Contains(t, msg, "docs/migrations/routing-config.md")
45	}

47	// TestLoadConfigWarnsOnceForOptInRoutingFields covers AC #2: configs
48	// containing profile_ladders + model_overrides load successfully but
49	// emit a one-time process warning, AND the default execute path does
50	// not consult those fields. Consultation only happens when --escalate /
51	// --override-model are explicitly passed.
52	func TestLoadConfigWarnsOnceForOptInRoutingFields(t *testing.T) {
53		ResetRoutingDeprecationWarnings()
54		t.Cleanup(ResetRoutingDeprecationWarnings)
55	
56		tempDir := t.TempDir()
57		ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
58		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
59		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
60	library:
61	  path: "./library"
62	  repository:
63	    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
64	    branch: "main"
65	agent:
66	  routing:
67	    profile_ladders:
68	      default: [cheap, standard, smart]
69	    model_overrides:
70	      cheap: qwen/qwen3.6
71	`), 0644))
72	
73		oldStderr := os.Stderr
74		r, w, err := os.Pipe()
75		require.NoError(t, err)
76		os.Stderr = w
77		t.Cleanup(func() { os.Stderr = oldStderr })
78	
79		cfg, loadErr := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
80		require.NoError(t, loadErr)
81		require.NotNil(t, cfg)
82	
83		// Calling load a second time must NOT re-emit the same warning.
84		_, _ = LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
85	
86		require.NoError(t, w.Close())
87		os.Stderr = oldStderr
88		out, err := io.ReadAll(r)
89		require.NoError(t, err)
90		stderrStr := string(out)
91	
92		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in")
93		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "agent.routing.model_overrides is opt-in")
94		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "--escalate")
95		assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "--override-model")
96		// Second load does not duplicate the warning lines.
97		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(stderrStr, "agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in"),
98			"profile_ladders warning must fire once per process")
99		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(stderrStr, "agent.routing.model_overrides is opt-in"),
100			"model_overrides warning must fire once per process")
101	
102		// Config still parses these fields so opt-in flags can consult them.
103		require.NotNil(t, cfg.Agent)
104		require.NotNil(t, cfg.Agent.Routing)
105		require.Contains(t, cfg.Agent.Routing.ProfileLadders, "default")
106		assert.Equal(t, "qwen/qwen3.6", cfg.Agent.Routing.ModelOverrides["cheap"])
107	}

1689	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoop(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
1690		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
1691		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
1692		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
1693		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
1694		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
1695		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
1696		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
1697		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
1698		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
1699		once, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("once")
1700		pollInterval, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("poll-interval")
1701		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
1702		local, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local")
1703		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
1704		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
1705		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
1706		minTier, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("min-tier")
1707		maxTier, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("max-tier")
1708		noAdaptiveMinTier, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-adaptive-min-tier")
1709		adaptiveWindow, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("adaptive-min-tier-window")
1710		maxCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-cost")
1711		escalate, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("escalate")
1712		overrideModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("override-model")
1713		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
1714	
1715		// Adaptive min-tier: when the operator did not pin --min-tier and adaptation
1716		// is not disabled, consult trailing cheap-tier success rate and promote to
1717		// standard when the cheap tier has been nearly pure waste. This saves the
1718		// queue from burning time and budget on attempts that almost never succeed.
1719		if minTier == "" && !noAdaptiveMinTier {
1720			adaptive := escalation.AdaptiveMinTier(projectRoot, adaptiveWindow, agent.ResolveModelTier)
1721			if adaptive.Skipped {
1722				minTier = string(adaptive.Tier)
1723				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
1724					"adaptive min-tier: skipping cheap tier (trailing success rate %.2f over %d attempts; threshold %.2f) — min-tier=%s\n",
1725					adaptive.CheapSuccessRate, adaptive.CheapAttempts, escalation.AdaptiveMinTierThreshold, minTier)
1726			}
1727		}
1728	
1729		// If --local, run inline; otherwise submit to running ddx server
1730		if !local {
1731			return f.executeLoopWithServer(cmd, projectRoot, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once, pollInterval, asJSON, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, minTier, maxTier, escalate)
1732		}
1733	
1734		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1735		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1736		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1737		// any bead status changes hands.
1738		if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {

25	type ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct {
26		// ProjectRoot overrides the manager's default project root for this worker.
27		// When set, the worker scans and executes beads from this project instead of
28		// the server's primary working directory. Must be an absolute path to a
29		// directory containing a .ddx/ folder. Validated by the server before the
30		// worker starts.
31		ProjectRoot  string        `json:"project_root,omitempty"`
32		Harness      string        `json:"harness,omitempty"`
33		Model        string        `json:"model,omitempty"`

44		ReviewModel   string `json:"review_model,omitempty"`
45		// Tier escalation bounds. Empty strings use the defaults (cheap and smart).
46		// Ignored when Harness or Model is pinned (escalation disabled).
47		MinTier string `json:"min_tier,omitempty"`
48		MaxTier string `json:"max_tier,omitempty"`
49		// Escalate opts into tier-ladder escalation semantics. Off by default:
50		// a single ResolveRoute call drives each attempt (matches --escalate on
51		// the CLI path). When false, profile_ladders and AdaptiveMinTier are
52		// not consulted.
