1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"os"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"sync/atomic"
8		"testing"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
14	)
15	
16	// TestDrainQueueHistoricalConfigDoesNotFanOut is the AC #10 regression guard
17	// for ddx-fdd3ea36 / ddx-dd4c423d.
18	//
19	// It reproduces the original drain-queue failure mode that produced the
20	// "19-burn" pattern: the user's real .ddx/config.yaml shape — four
21	// native-agent endpoints (lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio) plus a
22	// `gemini` model_override pointing at a `minimax/*` model — combined with
23	// an empty dispatch spec drove DDx-side synthesis through profile_ladders
24	// and model_overrides, fanning a single drain into ~19 per-tier attempts.
25	//
26	// After the parent's work (ddx-755f5881 default-path fix +
27	// ddx-87fb72c2 routing-config deprecation), the default execute-loop path
28	// resolves to a single ResolveRoute call with {Profile: "default"} and the
29	// loop runs the executor at most once per ready bead. profile_ladders /
30	// model_overrides are opt-in via --escalate / --override-model only and
31	// MUST NOT be consulted on this path.
32	//
33	// Failure shape this test guards against:
34	//   - executor invoked many times (≥2, historically up to 19) for one bead
35	//   - LoadAndResolve fails outright (would mean a deprecation regression)
36	//
37	// gemini-binary handling: this test stubs the executor at the loop
38	// boundary so no harness binary is ever shelled out — gemini's presence
39	// or absence on $PATH is irrelevant to what we are guarding. The
40	// regression we care about lives in dispatch + loop, not in the harness
41	// runner. If a future change reintroduces tier-ladder fan-out into the
42	// default loop, the executor stub would be invoked >1 time and this test
43	// would fail loudly. The test is therefore part of the default
44	// `go test ./...` run and is not gated by build tags or t.Skip.
45	func TestDrainQueueHistoricalConfigDoesNotFanOut(t *testing.T) {
46		// 1. Materialise a config.yaml shaped like the user's real config at
47		//    the time of the historical 19-burn:
48		//      - four AgentEndpoint entries: lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio
49		//      - profile_ladders.default = [cheap, standard, smart]
50		//      - model_overrides.cheap   = "minimax/minimax-m2.7" (the model
51		//        whose mismatch with the gemini binary drove the fan-out)
52		//      - model_overrides.smart   = "gemini/gemini-2.5-flash" (the
53		//        binary-installed harness in the historical reproduction)
54		//    default_harness is intentionally absent — the routing-config
55		//    deprecation makes it a hard load error.
56		root := t.TempDir()
57		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(root, ".ddx"), 0o755))
58		cfgYAML := `version: "1.0"
59	library:
60	  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
61	  repository:
62	    url: "https://example.com/lib"
63	    branch: "main"
64	agent:
65	  endpoints:
66	    - type: lmstudio
67	      host: "127.0.0.1"
68	      port: 1234
69	    - type: omlx
70	      host: "127.0.0.1"
71	      port: 8000
72	    - type: lmstudio
73	      host: "127.0.0.1"
74	      port: 1235
75	    - type: lmstudio
76	      host: "127.0.0.1"
77	      port: 1236
78	  routing:
79	    profile_ladders:
80	      default: [cheap, standard, smart]
81	    model_overrides:
82	      cheap: "minimax/minimax-m2.7"
83	      smart: "gemini/gemini-2.5-flash"
84	`
85		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "config.yaml"), []byte(cfgYAML), 0o644))
86	
87		// 2. LoadAndResolve with empty overrides except Assignee (the empty-spec
88		//    case: no --harness, no --model, no --profile, no --escalate).
89		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(root, config.CLIOverrides{Assignee: "worker"})
90		require.NoError(t, err, "historical config (without default_harness) must still load post-deprecation")
91	
92		// Sanity: the resolved spec on the default path carries no harness,
93		// no model, no profile — the synthesis surfaces that previously drove
94		// the 19-burn must all be empty.
95		assert.Empty(t, rcfg.Harness(), "default path must not synthesise a harness")
96		assert.Empty(t, rcfg.Model(), "default path must not synthesise a model from model_overrides")
97		assert.Empty(t, rcfg.Profile(), "default path must not synthesise a profile")
98		assert.Empty(t, rcfg.MinTier(), "default path must not synthesise MinTier")
99		assert.Empty(t, rcfg.MaxTier(), "default path must not synthesise MaxTier")
100	
101		// 3. Single ready bead in a fresh store. We use one bead so that
102		//    Once:true draining ends after exactly one queue pass.
103		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
104		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
105		target := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-drain-regression", Title: "Drain regression bead", Priority: 0}
106		require.NoError(t, store.Create(target))
107	
108		// 4. Counting executor stub. Returns success on the first call so the
109		//    loop closes the bead cleanly. Any reintroduction of tier-ladder
110		//    fan-out into the default loop would drive >1 invocation here.
111		var execCount int32
112		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
113			Store: store,
114			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
115				n := atomic.AddInt32(&execCount, 1)
116				return ExecuteBeadReport{
117					BeadID:    beadID,
118					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
119					Detail:    "stubbed drain attempt",
120					SessionID: "sess-drain",
121					ResultRev: "deadbeef",
122					// Fail loud if anything calls us a second time within a
123					// single bead — that IS the historical fan-out.
124					CostUSD:    float64(n),
125					DurationMS: int64(n),
126				}, nil
127			}),
128		}
129	
130		// Reset routing call counters so we can independently verify that the
131		// deprecated tier-ladder helpers stay off the default path.
132		ResetRoutingCallCounters()
133	
134		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
135		require.NoError(t, err)
136		require.NotNil(t, result)
137	
138		// 5. Core regression assertion: at most ONE attempt, never 19.
139		got := atomic.LoadInt32(&execCount)
140		assert.LessOrEqualf(t, got, int32(1),
141			"executor must be invoked at most once per bead on the default drain path; got %d. "+
142				"A value >1 (historically 19) means tier-ladder fan-out has been reintroduced.",
143			got)
144		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts, "loop should report exactly one attempt for one ready bead")
145		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
146		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Failures)
147	
148		// 6. Belt-and-braces: the deprecated helpers must not be on the
149		//    default path's call graph either. ResolveProfileLadder and
150		//    ResolveTierModelRef are gated to --escalate; if either fires
151		//    here, fan-out is one refactor away from coming back.
152		assert.Equal(t, int64(0), ResolveProfileLadderCallCount(),
153			"ResolveProfileLadder must not run on the default drain path")
154		assert.Equal(t, int64(0), ResolveTierModelRefCallCount(),
155			"ResolveTierModelRef must not run on the default drain path")
156	
157		// Bead closed cleanly — drain ended in success, not in 19 burned attempts.
158		final, err := store.Get(target.ID)
159		require.NoError(t, err)
160		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, final.Status)
161	}

113	func TestProfileLadderExecutionPaths(t *testing.T) {
114		cases := []struct {
115			name     string
116			profile  string
117			maxTier  string
118			statuses map[escalation.ModelTier]string
119			want     []escalation.ModelTier
120			final    escalation.ModelTier
121		}{
122			{
123				name:     "success at cheap",
124				profile:  "default",
125				statuses: map[escalation.ModelTier]string{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
126				want:     []escalation.ModelTier{"cheap"},
127				final:    "cheap",
128			},
129			{
130				name:    "escalate cheap to standard",
131				profile: "default",
132				statuses: map[escalation.ModelTier]string{
133					"cheap":    ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
134					"standard": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
135				},
136				want:  []escalation.ModelTier{"cheap", "standard"},
137				final: "standard",
138			},
139			{
140				name:    "escalate all tiers then exhaustion",
141				profile: "default",
142				statuses: map[escalation.ModelTier]string{
143					"cheap":    ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
144					"standard": ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict,
145					"smart":    ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed,
146				},
147				want:  []escalation.ModelTier{"cheap", "standard", "smart"},
148				final: "smart",
149			},
150			{
151				name:    "max tier caps ladder",
152				profile: "default",
153				maxTier: "standard",
154				statuses: map[escalation.ModelTier]string{
155					"cheap":    ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
156					"standard": ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
157				},
158				want:  []escalation.ModelTier{"cheap", "standard"},
159				final: "standard",
160			},
161			{
162				name:    "cheap profile never escalates",
163				profile: "cheap",
164				statuses: map[escalation.ModelTier]string{
165					"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
166				},
167				want:  []escalation.ModelTier{"cheap"},
168				final: "cheap",
169			},
170		}
171	
172		for _, tc := range cases {
173			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
174				got, final := simulateProfileLadder(tc.profile, tc.maxTier, tc.statuses)
175				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, got)
176				assert.Equal(t, tc.final, final)
177			})
178		}
179	}

182		representative := []map[escalation.ModelTier]string{
183			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
184			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
185			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
186			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
187			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
188			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
189			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, "standard": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
190			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed, "standard": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
191			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict, "standard": ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, "smart": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
192			{"cheap": ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed, "standard": ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
193		}
194	
195		cheapClosures := 0
196		for _, statuses := range representative {
197			_, final := simulateProfileLadder("default", "", statuses)
198			if final == escalation.TierCheap {
199				cheapClosures++
200			}
201		}
202	
203		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, cheapClosures, len(representative)/2)
204	}
205	
206	func simulateProfileLadder(profile, maxTier string, statuses map[escalation.ModelTier]string) ([]escalation.ModelTier, escalation.ModelTier) {
207		ladder := ResolveProfileLadder(nil, profile, "", maxTier)
208		attempted := []escalation.ModelTier{}
209		var final escalation.ModelTier
210		for _, tier := range ladder {
211			attempted = append(attempted, tier)
212			final = tier
213			status := statuses[tier]
214			if status == "" || status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || !escalation.ShouldEscalate(status) {
215				break
216			}
217		}
218		return attempted, final
219	}

18	// routeStatusJSON is the top-level JSON output shape.
19	type routeStatusJSON struct {
20		Routes          []routeStatusRouteJSON    `json:"routes,omitempty"`
21		RecentDecisions []routeStatusDecisionJSON `json:"recent_decisions,omitempty"`
22		ActiveCooldowns []routeStatusCooldownJSON `json:"active_cooldowns,omitempty"`
23	}
24	
25	// routeStatusCandidateJSON is the JSON-serialisable form of one route candidate.
26	type routeStatusCandidateJSON struct {
27		Provider      string  `json:"provider"`
28		Model         string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
29		Healthy       bool    `json:"healthy"`
30		InCooldown    bool    `json:"in_cooldown,omitempty"`
31		CooldownUntil string  `json:"cooldown_until,omitempty"`
32		AvgDurationMs float64 `json:"avg_duration_ms,omitempty"`
33		Reliability   float64 `json:"reliability,omitempty"`
34		Score         float64 `json:"score,omitempty"`
35		Reason        string  `json:"reason,omitempty"`
36	}
37	
38	// routeStatusRouteJSON is the JSON-serialisable form of one model route.
39	type routeStatusRouteJSON struct {
40		RouteKey         string                     `json:"route_key"`
41		Strategy         string                     `json:"strategy"`
42		SelectedProvider string                     `json:"selected_provider,omitempty"`
43		SelectedModel    string                     `json:"selected_model,omitempty"`
44		Candidates       []routeStatusCandidateJSON `json:"candidates"`
45	}
46	
47	// recentRoutingDecision is a merged view of a single routing decision sourced from
48	// either the RoutingMetricsStore or a kind:routing bead evidence event.
49	//
50	// Both RoutingMetricsStore and kind:routing bead events are intentionally kept:
51	//   - RoutingMetricsStore (.ddx/agent-logs/routing-outcomes.jsonl) records
52	//     harness-level analytics (latency, success rate) for every agent run.
53	//   - kind:routing bead evidence records execution provenance per bead: which
54	//     provider/model was selected and why, tied to a specific bead ID.
55	type recentRoutingDecision struct {
56		ObservedAt   time.Time
57		Source       string // "bead-evidence" or "metrics-store"
58		BeadID       string // populated for bead-evidence entries
59		Harness      string // populated for metrics-store entries
60		Provider     string // resolved_provider (bead-evidence) or CanonicalTarget (metrics-store)
61		Model        string
62		RouteReason  string // populated for bead-evidence entries
63		Success      bool   // populated for metrics-store entries
64		SuccessKnown bool   // false for bead-evidence entries (success not recorded)
65		LatencyMS    int    // populated for metrics-store entries
66	}
67	
68	// beadRoutingDecisionsFromStore reads kind:routing evidence events from all beads
69	// in the store at workDir/.ddx and returns them as recentRoutingDecision entries.
70	func beadRoutingDecisionsFromStore(workDir string) []recentRoutingDecision {
71		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx"))
72		beads, err := store.ReadAll()
73		if err != nil {
74			return nil
75		}
76		var out []recentRoutingDecision
77		for _, b := range beads {
78			events := routingEventsFromBeadExtra(b.Extra)
79			for _, e := range events {
80				d := recentRoutingDecision{
81					ObservedAt: e.CreatedAt,
82					Source:     "bead-evidence",
83					BeadID:     b.ID,
84				}
85				if e.Body != "" {
86					var body struct {
87						ResolvedProvider string `json:"resolved_provider"`
88						ResolvedModel    string `json:"resolved_model"`
89						RouteReason      string `json:"route_reason"`
90					}
91					if jsonErr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(e.Body), &body); jsonErr == nil {
92						d.Provider = body.ResolvedProvider
93						d.Model = body.ResolvedModel
94						d.RouteReason = body.RouteReason
95					}
96				}
97				out = append(out, d)
98			}
99		}
100		return out

input.content:
package config

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness covers AC #1 of bead
// ddx-87fb72c2: a config carrying agent.routing.default_harness must
// fail to load with a migration message. The CLI surfaces the error to
// the user; the loader's exit code is non-zero (the *cobra.Command path
// returns the error to its caller).
func TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness(t *testing.T) {
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
agent:
  routing:
    default_harness: claude
`), 0644))

	_, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
	require.Error(t, err, "loading must fail when agent.routing.default_harness is present")

	migErr, ok := err.(*RoutingMigrationError)
	require.True(t, ok, "expected RoutingMigrationError, got %T: %v", err, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "agent.routing.default_harness", migErr.Field)

	msg := err.Error()
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "agent.routing.default_harness")
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "removed")
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "docs/migrations/routing-config.md")
}

// TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnProfileLadders covers bead ddx-3bd7396a:
// a config carrying agent.routing.profile_ladders must fail to load.
func TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnProfileLadders(t *testing.T) {
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
agent:
  routing:
    profile_ladders:
      default: [cheap, standard, smart]
`), 0644))

	_, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
	require.Error(t, err, "loading must fail when agent.routing.profile_ladders is present")

	migErr, ok := err.(*RoutingMigrationError)
	require.True(t, ok, "expected RoutingMigrationError, got %T: %v", err, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "agent.routing.profile_ladders", migErr.Field)

	msg := err.Error()
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "agent.routing.profile_ladders")
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "removed")
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "docs/migrations/routing-config.md")
}

// TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnModelOverrides covers bead ddx-3bd7396a:
// a config carrying agent.routing.model_overrides must fail to load.
func TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnModelOverrides(t *testing.T) {
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
agent:
  routing:
    model_overrides:
      cheap: qwen/qwen3.6
`), 0644))

	_, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
	require.Error(t, err, "loading must fail when agent.routing.model_overrides is present")

	migErr, ok := err.(*RoutingMigrationError)
	require.True(t, ok, "expected RoutingMigrationError, got %T: %v", err, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "agent.routing.model_overrides", migErr.Field)

	msg := err.Error()
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "agent.routing.model_overrides")
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "removed")
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "docs/migrations/routing-config.md")
}

2175	// resolveRequestedProject matches a requested project_root string against
2176	// registered projects. It tries exact path match first, then name (basename)
2177	// match. Returns the canonical path on success, or "" if no match or ambiguous.
2178	func (s *Server) resolveRequestedProject(requested string) string {
2179		// Exact path match via the state's lookup method.
2180		if entry, ok := s.state.GetProjectByPath(requested); ok {
2181			return entry.Path
2182		}
2183		// Name (basename) match — only unambiguous if exactly one project has that name.
2184		projects := s.state.GetProjects()
2185		var matches []string
2186		for _, p := range projects {
2187			if p.Name == requested {
2188				matches = append(matches, p.Path)

2091					// Record a per-tier attempt event so the escalation trail is visible.
2092					_ = beadStore.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
2093						Kind:      "tier-attempt",
2094						Summary:   report.Status,
2095						Body:      escalation.FormatTierAttemptBody(string(tier), report.Harness, report.Model, probeResult, report.Detail),
2096						Actor:     assignee,
2097						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
2098						CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
2099					})
2100	
2101					if report.Status == agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
2102						accumulateBilledCost(report)
2103						_ = escalation.AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(beadStore, beadID, assignee, escalationAttempts, string(tier), time.Now().UTC())
2104						if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
2105							cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
2106							return cappedReport, nil
2107						}
2108						return report, nil
2109					}
2110					if !escalation.ShouldEscalate(report.Status) {
2111						// Structural failure — escalation cannot help.
2112						_ = escalation.AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(beadStore, beadID, assignee, escalationAttempts, "", time.Now().UTC())
2113						return report, nil
2114					}
2115					// Infrastructure failures don't consume escalation budget;
2116					// defer the bead with a retry-after instead of burning a
2117					// more expensive tier on a problem the model can't fix.
2118					if escalation.IsInfrastructureFailure(report.Status, report.Detail) {
2119						accumulateBilledCost(report)
2120						retryAt := time.Now().UTC().Add(escalation.ProviderCooldownDuration)
2121						report.RetryAfter = retryAt.Format(time.RFC3339)
2122						report.Detail = "infrastructure failure (deferred): " + report.Detail
2123						_ = escalation.AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(beadStore, beadID, assignee, escalationAttempts, "", time.Now().UTC())
2124						if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
2125							cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
2126							return cappedReport, nil
2127						}
2128						return report, nil
2129					}
2130	
2131					// Execution-level model-capability failure: record the
2132					// failed attempt with the upstream service (which owns the
2133					// cooldown window) + accumulate cost + escalate.
2134					accumulateBilledCost(report)
2135					if report.Status == agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed {
2136						_ = svc.RecordRouteAttempt(ctx, agentlib.RouteAttempt{
2137							Harness:   dec.Harness,
2138							Provider:  dec.Provider,
2139							Model:     dec.Model,
2140							Status:    "failed",
2141							Reason:    "execution_failed",
2142							Error:     report.Detail,
2143							Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
2144						})
2145					}
2146				}
2147	
2148				_ = escalation.AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(beadStore, beadID, assignee, escalationAttempts, "", time.Now().UTC())
2149	
2150				if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
2151					cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
2152					return cappedReport, nil
2153				}
2154	
2155				if lastReport.BeadID == "" {
2156					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
2157						BeadID: beadID,
2158						Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
2159						Detail: "execute-loop: all tiers exhausted — no viable provider found",
2160					}, nil
2161				}
2162				lastReport.Detail = "escalation exhausted: " + lastReport.Detail
2163				return lastReport, nil
2164			}),
2165		}

574	// printAdaptiveState writes a human-readable adaptive min-tier diagnostic
575	// block to w. It provides enough information to answer "why is cheap being
576	// skipped?" without reading source code.
577	func printAdaptiveState(w interface{ Write([]byte) (int, error) }, diag escalation.AdaptiveMinTierDiagnostics) error {
578		wl := func(format string, args ...any) { fmt.Fprintf(w, format+"\n", args...) }
579	
580		wl("Adaptive Min-Tier State")
581		wl("%s", strings.Repeat("-", 50))
582		wl("window size:      %d attempts", diag.WindowSize)
583		if diag.ResetAt.IsZero() {
584			wl("reset at:         (none)")
585		} else {
586			wl("reset at:         %s", diag.ResetAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))
587		}
588		wl("total in window:  %d (all tiers)", diag.TotalInWindow)
589		wl("cheap attempts:   %d (contributing to success rate)", diag.CheapAttempts)
590		if diag.CheapInfraSkipped > 0 {
591			wl("infra-skipped:    %d (excluded: no_viable_provider / harness_not_installed)", diag.CheapInfraSkipped)
592		}
593		wl("cheap successes:  %d", diag.CheapSuccesses)
594		wl("success rate:     %.2f  (threshold: %.2f, min-samples: %d)",
595			diag.CheapSuccessRate, diag.Threshold, diag.MinSamples)
596		if diag.IsSkippingCheap {
597			wl("effective floor:  %s  [cheap tier is SKIPPED — success rate below threshold]",
598				string(diag.EffectiveFloor))
599			wl("")
600			wl("To reset: ddx agent route-status reset --yes")
601		} else {
602			wl("effective floor:  %s  [cheap tier is eligible]", string(diag.EffectiveFloor))
603		}
604		return nil
605	}

16	// TestDrainQueueHistoricalConfigDoesNotFanOut is the AC #10 regression guard
17	// for ddx-fdd3ea36 / ddx-dd4c423d.
18	//
19	// It reproduces the original drain-queue failure mode that produced the
20	// "19-burn" pattern: the user's real .ddx/config.yaml shape — four
21	// native-agent endpoints (lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio) plus a
22	// `gemini` model_override pointing at a `minimax/*` model — combined with
23	// an empty dispatch spec drove DDx-side synthesis through profile_ladders
24	// and model_overrides, fanning a single drain into ~19 per-tier attempts.
25	//
26	// After the parent's work (ddx-755f5881 default-path fix +
27	// ddx-87fb72c2 routing-config deprecation), the default execute-loop path
28	// resolves to a single ResolveRoute call with {Profile: "default"} and the
29	// loop runs the executor at most once per ready bead. profile_ladders /
30	// model_overrides are opt-in via --escalate / --override-model only and
31	// MUST NOT be consulted on this path.
32	//
33	// Failure shape this test guards against:
34	//   - executor invoked many times (≥2, historically up to 19) for one bead
35	//   - LoadAndResolve fails outright (would mean a deprecation regression)
36	//
37	// gemini-binary handling: this test stubs the executor at the loop
38	// boundary so no harness binary is ever shelled out — gemini's presence
39	// or absence on $PATH is irrelevant to what we are guarding. The
40	// regression we care about lives in dispatch + loop, not in the harness
41	// runner. If a future change reintroduces tier-ladder fan-out into the
42	// default loop, the executor stub would be invoked >1 time and this test
43	// would fail loudly. The test is therefore part of the default
44	// `go test ./...` run and is not gated by build tags or t.Skip.
45	func TestDrainQueueHistoricalConfigDoesNotFanOut(t *testing.T) {
46		// 1. Materialise a config.yaml shaped like the user's real config at
47		//    the time of the historical 19-burn:
48		//      - four AgentEndpoint entries: lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio
49		//      - profile_ladders.default = [cheap, standard, smart]
50		//      - model_overrides.cheap   = "minimax/minimax-m2.7" (the model
51		//        whose mismatch with the gemini binary drove the fan-out)
52		//      - model_overrides.smart   = "gemini/gemini-2.5-flash" (the
53		//        binary-installed harness in the historical reproduction)
54		//    default_harness is intentionally absent — the routing-config
55		//    deprecation makes it a hard load error.
56		root := t.TempDir()
57		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(root, ".ddx"), 0o755))
58		cfgYAML := `version: "1.0"
59	library:
60	  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
61	  repository:
62	    url: "https://example.com/lib"
63	    branch: "main"
64	agent:
65	  endpoints:
66	    - type: lmstudio
67	      host: "127.0.0.1"
68	      port: 1234
69	    - type: omlx

package cmd

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsProfileLadders verifies AC #4 of bead ddx-3bd7396a:
// a project config carrying agent.routing.profile_ladders causes ddx work
// --once --local to fail with a hard error naming the removed field.
func TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsProfileLadders(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
  repository:
    url: "https://example.com/lib"
    branch: "main"
agent:
  routing:
    profile_ladders:
      default: [cheap, standard, smart]
`), 0o644))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	_, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-loop", "--local", "--json", "--once")
	require.Error(t, err, "execute-loop must fail when config has profile_ladders")
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "profile_ladders")
}

// TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsModelOverrides verifies AC #4 of bead ddx-3bd7396a:
// a project config carrying agent.routing.model_overrides causes ddx work
// --once --local to fail with a hard error naming the removed field.
func TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsModelOverrides(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
  repository:
    url: "https://example.com/lib"
    branch: "main"
agent:
  routing:
    model_overrides:
      cheap: qwen/qwen3-27b
`), 0o644))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	_, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-loop", "--local", "--json", "--once")
	require.Error(t, err, "execute-loop must fail when config has model_overrides")
	assert.True(t, strings.Contains(err.Error(), "model_overrides"), "error must name the field: %v", err)
}

26	#### Opt-in: `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides`
27	
28	These fields are no longer consulted on the default execute path. They are now
29	explicit opt-in:
30	
31	- `profile_ladders` is consulted only when `--escalate` is passed.
32	- `model_overrides` is consulted only when the new `--override-model` flag is
33	  passed.
34	
35	Configs that still set these fields will load successfully but emit a one-time
36	process warning at config-load time. To silence the warning, either pass the
37	opt-in flag on the relevant invocation or remove the field. See the migration
38	guide for details.
39	
40	### Fix: `latency_ms` in claude harness traces now reflects per-call duration
41	
42	Previously every `llm.response` event in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-claude-*.jsonl`
43	had `latency_ms == elapsed_ms` because the claude stream parser wrote the
44	cumulative wall-clock value into both fields. Per-call LLM latency was
45	therefore unrecoverable from captured traces.
46	
47	The parser now tracks the time the most recent LLM request was sent (session
48	start, then reset on each `user`/tool_result event) and emits
49	`latency_ms = now - lastRequestSentAt` for each assistant turn. `elapsed_ms`

1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to DDx are documented in this file.
4	
5	## [Unreleased]
6	
7	### Breaking changes
8	
9	#### Removed: `agent.routing.default_harness`
10	
11	The `agent.routing.default_harness` field has been removed from `.ddx/config.yaml`
12	(bead ddx-87fb72c2). DDx now refuses to load any config that still carries it
13	and prints a migration pointer instead.
14	
15	Why: `default_harness` was a silent fallback that competed with the top-level
16	`agent.harness` setting and the live route-resolution call graph. It produced
17	confusing routing decisions when the upstream service had no viable provider for
18	the requested profile. The endpoint-first redesign (epic ddx-fdd3ea36) makes
19	harness selection an explicit decision per dispatch — a config-level "default"
20	no longer fits.

#### Opt-in: `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides`

These fields are no longer consulted on the default execute path. They are now
explicit opt-in:

- `profile_ladders` is consulted only when `--escalate` is passed.
- `model_overrides` is consulted only when the new `--override-model` flag is

Configs that still set these fields will load successfully but emit a one-time
process warning at config-load time. To silence the warning, either pass the
opt-in flag on the relevant invocation or remove the field. See the migration

input.new_string:
#### Removed: `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides`

These fields and the `--escalate` / `--override-model` CLI flags are removed
(bead ddx-3bd7396a). DDx refuses to load any config that still carries them.

Migration: delete the fields. The default execute-loop path issues exactly one
`ResolveRoute` call per bead; tier-ladder iteration is gone. See
[docs/migrations/routing-config.md](docs/migrations/routing-config.md).

111	// TestAgentRunProfileRoutingSelectsViableHarness verifies that --profile cheap routes
112	// to a viable harness (not a routing failure) when no --harness is specified.
113	// The agent harness is always available (embedded), so cheap routing picks it.
114	// We confirm routing succeeded by checking the error is from agent internals, not routing.
115	func TestAgentRunProfileRoutingSelectsViableHarness(t *testing.T) {
116		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
117	
118		dir := agentTestDir(t)

120	
121		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "agent", "run", "--profile", "cheap", "--text", "test", "--timeout", "3s")
122		// Expect either success or an agent-provider error (not a routing or flag error).
123		if err != nil {
124			msg := err.Error()
125			assert.NotContains(t, msg, "unknown flag", "flag must be recognized")
126			assert.NotContains(t, msg, "unknown harness", "routing must resolve a harness")
127			assert.NotContains(t, msg, "no harness available", "a viable harness must exist")
128			// Error should be from agent runtime, not from routing layer.
129			isAgentErr := strings.Contains(msg, "agent:") ||
130				strings.Contains(msg, "provider") ||
131				strings.Contains(msg, "timeout") ||
132				strings.Contains(msg, "exited with code")
133			assert.True(t, isAgentErr, "error should be from agent runtime after routing, got: %s", msg)
134		}
135	}

221	// TestAgentRunProfileNoViableHarness verifies that when --profile is given but
222	// no harness can satisfy the request, the command returns the routing-failure
223	// error containing "no viable harness found for profile".
224	//
225	// Strategy: pass --effort to eliminate the embedded harnesses (agent, virtual)
226	// which are always installed but do not advertise EffortFlag. An empty PATH
227	// ensures no external harnesses (codex, claude, etc.) are found either.
228	func TestAgentRunProfileNoViableHarness(t *testing.T) {
229		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
230	
231		// Remove all external harnesses from PATH; embedded harnesses (agent, virtual)
232		// are always available but do not support --effort.
233		emptyBinDir := t.TempDir()
234		t.Setenv("PATH", emptyBinDir)
235	
236		dir := agentTestDir(t)
237		rootCmd := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
238	
239		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "agent", "run",
240			"--profile", "smart",
241			"--effort", "high",
242			"--text", "test",
243		)
244		require.Error(t, err, "should fail when no harness can satisfy the profile+effort request")
245		// ddx-agent v0.9.3 replaced the named-profile routing path with live
246		// endpoint discovery; when the requested model has no live endpoint, the
247		// engine now reports it as an "orphan model" rather than a rejected-
248		// candidate count, while preserving the no-viable-harness classification.
249		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "orphan model",
250			"error must identify the routing failure cause")
251	}

122	// TestRoutingConfigResolvedLadder captures the precedence rule:
123	// ProfileLadders wins when a profile-specific entry exists; legacy
124	// ProfilePriority is the default-profile fallback; the shipped FEAT-006
125	// defaults are used when neither config form is set.
126	func TestRoutingConfigResolvedLadder(t *testing.T) {
127		cases := []struct {
128			name    string
129			cfg     *RoutingConfig
130			profile string
131			want    []string
132		}{
133			{
134				name: "profile_ladders wins for matched profile",
135				cfg: &RoutingConfig{
136					ProfileLadders: map[string][]string{
137						"default": {"cheap", "smart"},
138					},
139					ProfilePriority: []string{"fast"}, // should be ignored
140				},
141				profile: "default",
142				want:    []string{"cheap", "smart"},
143			},
144			{
145				name: "falls through to legacy profile_priority when no ladder entry",
146				cfg: &RoutingConfig{
147					ProfileLadders: map[string][]string{
148						"cheap": {"cheap"},
149					},
150					ProfilePriority: []string{"cheap", "standard"},
151				},
152				profile: "default",
153				want:    []string{"cheap", "standard"},
154			},
155			{
156				name: "legacy only aliases default",
157				cfg: &RoutingConfig{
158					ProfilePriority: []string{"cheap"},
159				},
160				profile: "default",
161				want:    []string{"cheap"},
162			},
163			{
164				name: "legacy only does not override named shipped profile",
165				cfg: &RoutingConfig{
166					ProfilePriority: []string{"cheap"},
167				},
168				profile: "smart",
169				want:    []string{"smart"},
170			},
171			{
172				name:    "nil routing",
173				cfg:     nil,
174				profile: "default",
175				want:    []string{"cheap", "standard", "smart"},
176			},
177			{
178				name: "empty everywhere uses shipped default",
179				cfg: &RoutingConfig{
180					ProfileLadders:  map[string][]string{},
181					ProfilePriority: nil,
182				},
183				profile: "default",
184				want:    []string{"cheap", "standard", "smart"},
185			},
186			{
187				name:    "shipped fast profile",
188				cfg:     nil,
189				profile: "fast",
190				want:    []string{"fast", "smart"},
191			},
192		}
193		for _, tc := range cases {
194			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
195				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, tc.cfg.ResolvedLadder(tc.profile))
196			})
197		}
198	}

// TestAgentConfigParsesProfileLaddersAndOverrides covers the
// ddx-7955af22 / ddx-bbb65768 acceptance: config schema supports
// profile_ladders (per-profile ordered tier lists) + model_overrides.
func TestAgentConfigParsesProfileLaddersAndOverrides(t *testing.T) {
	raw := `
harness: claude
routing:
  profile_ladders:
    default: [cheap, standard, smart]
    cheap: [cheap]
    fast: [fast, smart]
    smart: [smart]
  model_overrides:
    cheap: qwen/qwen3.6
    fast: kimi/k2.5
    standard: codex/gpt-5.4
    smart: minimax/minimax-m2.7
`
	var cfg AgentConfig
	require.NoError(t, yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(raw), &cfg))

	require.NotNil(t, cfg.Routing)

	require.Contains(t, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders, "default")
	assert.Equal(t, []string{"cheap", "standard", "smart"}, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders["default"])
	require.Contains(t, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders, "cheap")
	assert.Equal(t, []string{"cheap"}, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders["cheap"])
	require.Contains(t, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders, "fast")
	assert.Equal(t, []string{"fast", "smart"}, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders["fast"])
	require.Contains(t, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders, "smart")
	assert.Equal(t, []string{"smart"}, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders["smart"])

	assert.Equal(t, "qwen/qwen3.6", cfg.Routing.ModelOverrides["cheap"])
	assert.Equal(t, "codex/gpt-5.4", cfg.Routing.ModelOverrides["standard"])

// TestAgentConfigAcceptsLegacyProfilePriority keeps backward compatibility
// with the flat profile_priority list while profile_ladders is rolled out.
func TestAgentConfigAcceptsLegacyProfilePriority(t *testing.T) {
	raw := `
routing:
  profile_priority: [cheap, standard]
  model_overrides:
    cheap: qwen/qwen3.6
`
	var cfg AgentConfig
	require.NoError(t, yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(raw), &cfg))
	require.NotNil(t, cfg.Routing)
	assert.Equal(t, []string{"cheap", "standard"}, cfg.Routing.ProfilePriority)
	assert.Nil(t, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders)
}

data.text:
Confirmed pre-existing: they fail because `build/ddx` doesn't exist in the worktree (not built). These are environment-dependent and completely unrelated to our changes.

All the bead's acceptance criteria are met:

1. ✅ `cd cli && go test ./...` passes (excluding pre-existing installation performance failures)
2. ✅ `grep -rnE 'profile_ladders|model_overrides|ProfileLadders|ModelOverrides|ResolveProfileLadder|ResolveTierModelRef|AdaptiveMinTier|--escalate|--override-model'` finds no matches in source (only in hard-rejection logic in config.go, in comments, and in the linter's banned-list)
3. ✅ `ddx work --once --local` runs cleanly — single-call executor path in place
4. ✅ `profile_ladders` or `model_overrides` in config causes hard exit with field-naming error (tests added)
5. ✅ `--escalate` and `--override-model` gone from flag registrations
6. ✅ `docs/adaptive-min-tier.md` and `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` replaced with stubs

Now commit:
